mnemonics.title: Backward Chaining Flow
mnemonics.content: GOAL: Start with What You Want → Find Rules → Check Facts → Repeat → Prove or Fail → Backtrack if Needed. Mnemonic: G-R-C-F-R-P-F-B (Goal Rules Check Facts Repeat Prove Fail Backtrack)

mnemonics.title: DFS in Backward Chaining
mnemonics.content: Depth-First Strategy: Dive Deep → Hit Dead End? → Backtrack → Try Next Branch → Continue Until Solved or Exhausted.

mnemonics.title: Horn Clause Memory
mnemonics.content: Horn = 'One Positive' → Head ← Body → Think: HORN = HO = Head Only positive. Rule: A ← B ∧ C means 'A if B and C'.

acronyms.usage: Primary search strategy used in backward chaining to explore rule chains
Goal stack operates as LIFO—most recent goal is processed first
Resolution strategy used in Prolog for backward chaining
Abstract machine implementing Prolog's backward chaining execution

visualTips.visualization: Draw an upside-down tree: Root at top = MAIN GOAL, branches = sub-goals from rules, leaves at bottom = KNOWN FACTS. Think: Roots grow down, so goal at top!
Stack diagram: Push goals on top, pop when solved. If fail, look at next item below. LIFO = Last goal pushed = First goal to solve.
Two puzzle pieces clicking together = unification. Variables are blank spaces that get filled with matching values from facts.
Path in a maze: Dead end → retrace steps → try different corridor. Same with rules: failed path → go back → try alternative rule.

analogies.analogy: Like a detective starting with 'Who committed the crime?' (goal) and working backwards: motive → opportunity → alibi → witnesses → evidence.
Think of SAT-nav: You enter destination first (goal), then system finds routes from your current location (facts) to reach that destination.
Solving a maze by following one path until hitting a wall, then returning to the last junction and trying a different path.
Family tree reversed—grandparents at bottom (facts), parents in middle (sub-goals), child at top (main goal). Child exists only if parents exist.
One-way street: Cars (facts/rules) can only enter from one direction. One conclusion (head) from multiple conditions (body).
